name: broken-authentication
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to \"test for broken authentication vulnerabilities\", \"assess session management security\", \"perform credential stuffing tests\", \"evaluate ...
capabilities: Assess password policy enforcement and identify weak password vulnerabilities
Test for session token predictability and insufficient entropy
Evaluate account lockout mechanisms and rate limiting controls
Identify username enumeration through differential response analysis
Analyze password reset token generation and expiration
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a legitimate security education skill for testing authentication vulnerabilities. All static findings are false positives - the skill contains documentation and examples showing testing methodologies, not malicious code. The skill explicitly requires written authorization and is designed for authorized security testing professionals.
valueStatement: Identify broken authentication vulnerabilities in web applications using systematic testing methodologies. This skill guides security professionals through password policy assessment, session management testing, and credential stuffing analysis.
limitations: Requires explicit written authorization before testing
Cannot test applications without valid test accounts
CAPTCHA and advanced rate limiting may limit automated testing
Third-party authentication providers cannot be tested directly

useCases.title: Pre-deployment Security Assessment
useCases.description: Test authentication mechanisms in staging environments before production release to identify vulnerabilities

useCases.targetUser: Penetration testers and red team members
useCases.title: Red Team Engagement
useCases.description: Evaluate client authentication security as part of authorized penetration testing engagements

useCases.title: Security Training and Education
useCases.description: Learn authentication vulnerability testing methodologies for professional development

promptTemplates.title: Basic Password Policy Test
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me test the password policy of our login system. What password requirements should I check for and how can I identify weaknesses in the policy enforcement?

promptTemplates.title: Session Token Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to analyze session tokens from our application. What characteristics should I look for to determine if session tokens are secure? How do I test for predictability?
promptTemplates.scenario: Evaluating session token entropy and randomness

promptTemplates.title: Account Lockout Testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Guide me through testing account lockout mechanisms. What are the test steps to verify lockout works correctly and what bypass techniques should I check for?

promptTemplates.title: MFA Implementation Review
promptTemplates.prompt: What security checks should I perform when testing multi-factor authentication implementation? How do I identify common MFA bypass vulnerabilities?

bestPractices: Always obtain written authorization before testing any system
Use dedicated test accounts rather than real user credentials
Document all findings with proof-of-concept evidence
antiPatterns: Testing production systems without explicit authorization
Using real breached credentials from actual data breaches
Attempting to access accounts belonging to other users

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes, this is a legitimate security education skill. It explicitly requires written authorization and is designed for authorized security testing professionals.

faq.question: What tools does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill references common security testing tools like Burp Suite, Hydra, and custom wordlists. These are standard tools used by professional penetration testers.

faq.question: Do I need special permissions to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you must have explicit written authorization from the system owner before testing. This is a legal requirement for legitimate security testing.

faq.question: Can this skill test any website?
faq.answer: No. You must only test systems you own or have explicit authorization to test. Unauthorized access is illegal and unethical.

faq.question: What authentication methods can be tested?
faq.answer: The skill covers password-based authentication, token-based auth (JWT, OAuth), session management, multi-factor authentication, and password reset flows.

faq.question: How long does a typical assessment take?
faq.answer: Assessment time varies based on application complexity. A thorough authentication review typically takes 2-8 hours depending on scope and testing depth.
